items.text:
Recreation Center Weekend Hours Update

Beginning Saturday, October 4, the Hillcrest Recreation Center will be open from 8:00 AM to 6:00 PM on weekends. The earlier closing time allows staff to complete scheduled maintenance on the pool and the weight room.

Please note the following changes:
- A valid student ID must be scanned at the front desk for entry.
- Guest passes are available for $5 each and must be purchased before 4:00 PM.
- The indoor track will be reserved for team practice from 8:00 AM to 10:00 AM.
- Lockers are cleared every night, and personal items left overnight will be donated.

Members with questions may contact the facilities office at the second-floor desk.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what must members do to enter the center?

options.A: Purchase a guest pass at the front desk.
options.B: Scan a valid student ID at the front desk.
options.C: Reserve the indoor track before arriving.
options.D: Contact the facilities office in advance.

questions.explanation: The notice states that a valid student ID must be scanned at the front desk for entry, which matches option B.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this notice?

options.A: To announce updated weekend hours and related entry rules.
options.B: To advertise a discount on weekend guest passes.
options.C: To recruit volunteers for pool maintenance work.
options.D: To explain how students can join a sports team.

questions.explanation: The notice's central message is the new weekend schedule and the accompanying rules for entry, passes, and the track, so option A captures its overall purpose.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about personal items left in the lockers overnight?

options.A: They will not be returned to their owners.
options.B: They will be stored safely at the front desk.
options.C: They will be labeled and held for one week.
options.D: They will require a $5 fee to retrieve.

questions.explanation: Because the notice says lockers are cleared every night and items left overnight will be donated, we can infer that such items will not be returned to their owners, making A correct.
